source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Recall multiplication and division facts for multiplication tables up to 12 x 12.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack focuses on mixed recall, structure and repair for 6, 7, 8, 9 and 12 table facts.

objective.child_goal: I can use patterns to remember tricky multiplication facts and explain them to my companion.
objective.statement: Recall multiplication and division facts for multiplication tables up to 12 x 12.
misconceptions: Confuses nearby multiplication facts such as 6 x 8 and 7 x 8.
Counts every fact from one instead of using known facts.
Does not connect division facts to multiplication facts.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A number you multiply by another number.
vocabulary.audio_script: A factor is one of the numbers in a multiplication fact.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two glowing input crystals feeding one product machine.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The answer when two factors are multiplied.
vocabulary.audio_script: The product is the result made by the factors.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: The powered total shown on the machine display.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Power three quick facts your companion has nearly remembered.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Retrieve prerequisite facts without high pressure.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Let's warm up the engine with facts you already know.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three small energy cells orbit the companion.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The forge needs equal rows of energy cells. Equal rows make multiplication faster.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Set multiplication inside a meaningful visual context.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Equal rows help us see a multiplication fact all at once.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Rows of cells snap into an array.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: 7 x 8 can be split into 5 x 8 and 2 x 8. That is 40 and 16, so the product is 56.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Show decomposition rather than rote-only recall.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Break seven rows into five rows and two rows. Forty plus sixteen is fifty-six.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: An array separates into 5 rows and 2 rows, then recombines.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build 7 rows of 8, then tap the two parts that help you remember the fact.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Move from visual structure to recall strategy.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Build it first. Then choose the helpful parts.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Drag row blocks into an array grid.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which fact helps you check 7 x 8: 5 x 8 plus 2 x 8, or 7 + 8?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect additive confusion before independent practice.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Choose the helper fact that keeps equal groups.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two possible routes appear on a control panel.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Show your companion why 8 x 7 is the same product as 7 x 8.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation as retrieval and evidence.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you teach me how swapping the factors keeps the product?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The array rotates while the product remains fixed.

manipulatives.purpose: Show equal groups, decomposition and commutativity for multiplication facts.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children drag row blocks, split arrays and connect products to division facts.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard controls select row count and column count; low-sensory mode uses simple snap states.

manipulatives.purpose: Secure multiplication recall by matching a known product to both related division facts.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners dock multiplication and division fact cards around one product planet, reject distractors with the wrong divisor or quotient and explain how the inverse facts verify the table fact.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard shortcuts and move menus dock cards without dragging; single-switch scanning cycles through cards, slots, check and undo with no speed pressure; static mode presents a product-centred fact-family table; reduced-motion mode removes orbiting and docking animation, using instant outlines and spoken equation confirmations.

practice.interleaving_notes: Mix multiplication with related division after two successful recall rounds; include area links once secure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Cover multiplication facts up to 12 x 12 across mixed order, commutative pairs and known difficult facts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: factor pair + operation prompt + adaptive time window + close numeric distractors for choice mode
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include 6x8, 7x8, 8x7, 9x7 and related facts with extra misconception tagging.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Teach efficient decomposition using arrays so recall is connected to structure.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: target fact + split strategy + interactive row/column builder
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Curriculum review must ensure every decomposition is mathematically efficient and age appropriate.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Link multiplication facts to division facts and missing-factor problems.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: fact family + missing factor + matching division statement
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check that division prompts stay tied to known multiplication families before mixed challenge.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Apply multiplication facts to area, equal groups and scaling contexts without changing the core objective.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: context scene + rectangular array + target fact + reasoning prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep language concise; this is transfer practice, not a new area objective.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Supply spaced retrieval variants for 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 day review queues.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review scheduler selects prior weak facts, alternates multiplication and division
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Adaptive engine should weight prior error history and not simply randomise tables.

body.prompt: Power the forge: 7 x 8 = ?

interaction_route.touch: Tap labelled rows, columns, groups, fact-family cards or a numeric/choice response; precise array dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the array or fact family; use row/column steppers, number entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, known-fact/array model, controls or choices, check and retry in a fixed order with one activation per decision.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select factor, group, fact-family and response targets with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to set rows/groups or record the indicated answer without the adult supplying the fact or strategy.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent multiplication evidence; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.array_equal_groups_route: A labelled rows-by-columns array, equal-group trays and a linear repeated-groups table show every group exactly once without colour-only meaning.
body.decomposition_route: Known anchor arrays and complete-row splits remain visible; partial products recombine to the unchanged whole array.
body.fact_family_route: One product is centred with two multiplication and two inverse division facts; missing factors can be selected, typed, pointed to or adult-scribed.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal one known fact, complete group, decomposition part or inverse step at a time while preserving correct groups and products.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

question_variants.explanation: 7 x 8 is 56 because 5 rows of 8 is 40 and 2 more rows of 8 is 16.

feedback.correct: “56” matches the multiplication fact and can be checked with an equal-groups array, a known-fact adjustment or the commutative fact.
feedback.repair: Label both factors, identify which nearby fact the first product belongs to, then add or remove one complete equal group and check the adjusted product with an array.
feedback.misconception_check: Identify which nearby fact the wrong product actually represents.
feedback.representation_evidence: 7 equal groups of 8 make 56; turning the array gives 8 groups of 7 with the same product.
feedback.check_prompt: Which exact two factors are shown, which nearby fact did the other product represent, and how many complete groups must change?
feedback.strategy_support: Use EXACT FACTORS → KNOWN ANCHOR/COMMUTATIVE ARRAY → ADD OR REMOVE COMPLETE GROUPS → INVERSE OR ARRAY CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

hints: 8 x 7 has the same product as 7 x 8.
Rotate the array in your mind.
question_variants.explanation: 8 x 7 and 7 x 8 both make 56 because the same array can be turned around.

feedback.correct: “56” matches the multiplication fact and can be checked with an equal-groups array, a known-fact adjustment or the commutative fact.
feedback.repair: Build or imagine the same rectangular array, turn it so rows and columns swap, and check that the number of cells—and therefore the product—does not change.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the array structure and fact family.
feedback.representation_evidence: 8 x 7 and 7 x 8 both make 56 because the same array can be turned around. The array/equal-groups or fact-family model preserves the same product 56.
feedback.check_prompt: If the array is turned, are all the same cells still present once, with only rows and columns swapped?
feedback.strategy_support: Use EXACT FACTORS → KNOWN ANCHOR/COMMUTATIVE ARRAY → ADD OR REMOVE COMPLETE GROUPS → INVERSE OR ARRAY CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

interaction_route.touch: Tap labelled rows, columns, groups, fact-family cards or a numeric/choice response; precise array dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the array or fact family; use row/column steppers, number entry and Enter or Space to select and check.

Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

question_variants.explanation: 56 divided into 8 equal rows gives 7 in each row because 7 x 8 = 56.

feedback.correct: “7” completes the equal groups and the inverse fact: 56 ÷ 8 = 7, checked by 8 × 7 = 56.
feedback.repair: Rewrite the division as divisor × ? = dividend, build or reveal equal groups, find the missing factor, then multiply to check the quotient.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the array structure and fact family.
feedback.representation_evidence: 56 ÷ 8 = 7 because 8 × 7 = 56; the multiplication and division equations describe the same equal groups.
feedback.check_prompt: What multiplication fact has 56 as its product and the shown divisor as one factor?
feedback.strategy_support: Use DIVIDEND ÷ DIVISOR → DIVISOR × ? = DIVIDEND → EQUAL GROUPS → MULTIPLICATION CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

question_variants.explanation: 6 x 7 is 42. It can be checked from 5 x 7 by adding 1 more group of 7.

feedback.correct: “42” matches the multiplication fact and can be checked with an equal-groups array, a known-fact adjustment or the commutative fact.
feedback.repair: Label both factors, identify which nearby fact the first product belongs to, then add or remove one complete equal group and check the adjusted product with an array.
feedback.misconception_check: Identify which nearby fact the wrong product actually represents.
feedback.representation_evidence: 6 equal groups of 7 make 42; turning the array gives 7 groups of 6 with the same product.
feedback.check_prompt: Which exact two factors are shown, which nearby fact did the other product represent, and how many complete groups must change?
feedback.strategy_support: Use EXACT FACTORS → KNOWN ANCHOR/COMMUTATIVE ARRAY → ADD OR REMOVE COMPLETE GROUPS → INVERSE OR ARRAY CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

body.prompt: Power the next forge cell: 6 x 8 = ?

interaction_route.touch: Tap labelled rows, columns, groups, fact-family cards or a numeric/choice response; precise array dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the array or fact family; use row/column steppers, number entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, known-fact/array model, controls or choices, check and retry in a fixed order with one activation per decision.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select factor, group, fact-family and response targets with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to set rows/groups or record the indicated answer without the adult supplying the fact or strategy.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent multiplication evidence; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.array_equal_groups_route: A labelled rows-by-columns array, equal-group trays and a linear repeated-groups table show every group exactly once without colour-only meaning.
body.decomposition_route: Known anchor arrays and complete-row splits remain visible; partial products recombine to the unchanged whole array.
body.fact_family_route: One product is centred with two multiplication and two inverse division facts; missing factors can be selected, typed, pointed to or adult-scribed.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal one known fact, complete group, decomposition part or inverse step at a time while preserving correct groups and products.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select factor, group, fact-family and response targets with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to set rows/groups or record the indicated answer without the adult supplying the fact or strategy.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent multiplication evidence; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.array_equal_groups_route: A labelled rows-by-columns array, equal-group trays and a linear repeated-groups table show every group exactly once without colour-only meaning.
body.decomposition_route: Known anchor arrays and complete-row splits remain visible; partial products recombine to the unchanged whole array.
body.fact_family_route: One product is centred with two multiplication and two inverse division facts; missing factors can be selected, typed, pointed to or adult-scribed.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal one known fact, complete group, decomposition part or inverse step at a time while preserving correct groups and products.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

question_variants.explanation: 7 x 7 is 49. It can be checked from 5 x 7 by adding 2 more groups of 7.

feedback.correct: “49” matches the multiplication fact and can be checked with an equal-groups array, a known-fact adjustment or the commutative fact.
feedback.repair: Label both factors, identify which nearby fact the first product belongs to, then add or remove one complete equal group and check the adjusted product with an array.
feedback.misconception_check: Identify which nearby fact the wrong product actually represents.
feedback.representation_evidence: 7 equal groups of 7 make 49; turning the array gives 7 groups of 7 with the same product.
feedback.check_prompt: Which exact two factors are shown, which nearby fact did the other product represent, and how many complete groups must change?
feedback.strategy_support: Use EXACT FACTORS → KNOWN ANCHOR/COMMUTATIVE ARRAY → ADD OR REMOVE COMPLETE GROUPS → INVERSE OR ARRAY CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

feedback.repair: Label both factors, identify which nearby fact the first product belongs to, then add or remove one complete equal group and check the adjusted product with an array.
feedback.misconception_check: Identify which nearby fact the wrong product actually represents.
feedback.representation_evidence: 8 equal groups of 7 make 56; turning the array gives 7 groups of 8 with the same product.
feedback.check_prompt: Which exact two factors are shown, which nearby fact did the other product represent, and how many complete groups must change?
feedback.strategy_support: Use EXACT FACTORS → KNOWN ANCHOR/COMMUTATIVE ARRAY → ADD OR REMOVE COMPLETE GROUPS → INVERSE OR ARRAY CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

body.prompt: Power the next forge cell: 8 x 8 = ?

interaction_route.touch: Tap labelled rows, columns, groups, fact-family cards or a numeric/choice response; precise array dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the array or fact family; use row/column steppers, number entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, known-fact/array model, controls or choices, check and retry in a fixed order with one activation per decision.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select factor, group, fact-family and response targets with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to set rows/groups or record the indicated answer without the adult supplying the fact or strategy.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent multiplication evidence; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.array_equal_groups_route: A labelled rows-by-columns array, equal-group trays and a linear repeated-groups table show every group exactly once without colour-only meaning.
body.decomposition_route: Known anchor arrays and complete-row splits remain visible; partial products recombine to the unchanged whole array.
body.fact_family_route: One product is centred with two multiplication and two inverse division facts; missing factors can be selected, typed, pointed to or adult-scribed.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal one known fact, complete group, decomposition part or inverse step at a time while preserving correct groups and products.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the array or fact family; use row/column steppers, number entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, known-fact/array model, controls or choices, check and retry in a fixed order with one activation per decision.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select factor, group, fact-family and response targets with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to set rows/groups or record the indicated answer without the adult supplying the fact or strategy.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent multiplication evidence; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.array_equal_groups_route: A labelled rows-by-columns array, equal-group trays and a linear repeated-groups table show every group exactly once without colour-only meaning.
body.decomposition_route: Known anchor arrays and complete-row splits remain visible; partial products recombine to the unchanged whole array.
body.fact_family_route: One product is centred with two multiplication and two inverse division facts; missing factors can be selected, typed, pointed to or adult-scribed.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal one known fact, complete group, decomposition part or inverse step at a time while preserving correct groups and products.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

hints: Use the known 10 times table fact.
question_variants.explanation: 9 x 7 is 63. It can be checked from 10 x 7. The expected response is 63. Use the known 10 times table fact. 10 x 7 = 70.

feedback.correct: “63” matches the multiplication fact and can be checked with an equal-groups array, a known-fact adjustment or the commutative fact.
feedback.repair: Label both factors, identify which nearby fact the first product belongs to, then add or remove one complete equal group and check the adjusted product with an array.
feedback.misconception_check: Identify which nearby fact the wrong product actually represents.
feedback.representation_evidence: 9 equal groups of 7 make 63; turning the array gives 7 groups of 9 with the same product.
feedback.check_prompt: Which exact two factors are shown, which nearby fact did the other product represent, and how many complete groups must change?
feedback.strategy_support: Use EXACT FACTORS → KNOWN ANCHOR/COMMUTATIVE ARRAY → ADD OR REMOVE COMPLETE GROUPS → INVERSE OR ARRAY CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the array or fact family; use row/column steppers, number entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, known-fact/array model, controls or choices, check and retry in a fixed order with one activation per decision.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select factor, group, fact-family and response targets with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to set rows/groups or record the indicated answer without the adult supplying the fact or strategy.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent multiplication evidence; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.array_equal_groups_route: A labelled rows-by-columns array, equal-group trays and a linear repeated-groups table show every group exactly once without colour-only meaning.
body.decomposition_route: Known anchor arrays and complete-row splits remain visible; partial products recombine to the unchanged whole array.
body.fact_family_route: One product is centred with two multiplication and two inverse division facts; missing factors can be selected, typed, pointed to or adult-scribed.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal one known fact, complete group, decomposition part or inverse step at a time while preserving correct groups and products.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

question_variants.explanation: 6 equal rows of 8 contain 48 cells because 6 x 8 = 48.

feedback.correct: “48” counts every cell in 6 equal rows of 8; the complete array represents the multiplication product.
feedback.repair: Keep the shown known-fact rows, add only the missing complete groups, and recombine instead of recounting every cell from one.
feedback.misconception_check: Use the known anchor instead of recounting every group from one.
feedback.representation_evidence: 6 rows × 8 in each row = 48. Every cell is counted once.
feedback.check_prompt: Which known rows can stay, and exactly which complete rows must be added to reach the target array?
feedback.strategy_support: Use LABEL ROWS/COLUMNS → KEEP EQUAL GROUP SIZE → SPLIT INTO KNOWN ARRAYS → RECOMBINE → CHECK PRODUCT.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

body.array_equal_groups_route: A labelled rows-by-columns array, equal-group trays and a linear repeated-groups table show every group exactly once without colour-only meaning.
body.decomposition_route: Known anchor arrays and complete-row splits remain visible; partial products recombine to the unchanged whole array.
body.fact_family_route: One product is centred with two multiplication and two inverse division facts; missing factors can be selected, typed, pointed to or adult-scribed.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal one known fact, complete group, decomposition part or inverse step at a time while preserving correct groups and products.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

hints: Use the matching multiplication fact 12 x 10 = 120.
question_variants.explanation: 120 divided into 12 equal rows gives 10 in each row because 12 x 10 = 120.

feedback.correct: “10” completes the equal groups and the inverse fact: 120 ÷ 12 = 10, checked by 12 × 10 = 120.
feedback.repair: Rewrite the division as divisor × ? = dividend, build or reveal equal groups, find the missing factor, then multiply to check the quotient.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the array structure and fact family.
feedback.representation_evidence: 120 ÷ 12 = 10 because 12 × 10 = 120; the multiplication and division equations describe the same equal groups.
feedback.check_prompt: What multiplication fact has 120 as its product and the shown divisor as one factor?
feedback.strategy_support: Use DIVIDEND ÷ DIVISOR → DIVISOR × ? = DIVIDEND → EQUAL GROUPS → MULTIPLICATION CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct groups and known facts, open one model or check prompt, then retry without losing progress or speed points.

question_variants.explanation: 6 rows of 7 contain 42 cells. The split 5 × 7 plus 1 × 7 recombines the same array.

feedback.correct: Relay secured using array product build.
feedback.repair: Keep the row size fixed, show every row exactly once and recombine the split array.
feedback.misconception_check: Use the known anchor instead of recounting every group from one.

mission.campaign: Forge Network: Restore the Twelve Constellation Relays
mission.objective: Complete the repair nearby fact confusion phase and verify the relay value.
mission.strategic_tool: identify the nearby fact and restore the missing group
mission.verification: check with a turned or split array

reward.earned_for: using a valid strategy or completing a repair
reward.effect: lights a route on the forge map without changing difficulty or adding pressure

mission.retry_protocol: No energy, lives or progress are lost; the mission preserves correct groups and reveals a targeted strategy clue.

interaction_route.touch: Tap labelled rows, columns, groups, fact-family cards or a numeric/choice response; precise array dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the array or fact family; use row/column steppers, number entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, known-fact/array model, controls or choices, check and retry in a fixed order with one activation per decision.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select factor, group, fact-family and response targets with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to set rows/groups or record the indicated answer without the adult supplying the fact or strategy.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent multiplication evidence; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.array_equal_groups_route: A labelled rows-by-columns array, equal-group trays and a linear repeated-groups table show every group exactly once without colour-only meaning.
body.decomposition_route: Known anchor arrays and complete-row splits remain visible; partial products recombine to the unchanged whole array.
body.fact_family_route: One product is centred with two multiplication and two inverse division facts; missing factors can be selected, typed, pointed to or adult-scribed.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal one known fact, complete group, decomposition part or inverse step at a time while preserving correct groups and products.

question_variants.explanation: 88 is the nearby fact 11 × 8. Adding the missing group of 8 gives 96.

feedback.correct: Relay secured using spaced misconception repair.
feedback.repair: Name the nearby fact, then restore the missing group shown in 88 + 8 = 96.
feedback.misconception_check: Identify which nearby fact the wrong product actually represents.
feedback.retry: The forge keeps your correct steps. Choose another strategy and continue without a timer or penalty.
feedback.representation_evidence: 12 equal groups of 8 make 96; turning the array gives 8 groups of 12 with the same product.
feedback.check_prompt: Which exact two factors are shown, which nearby fact did the other product represent, and how many complete groups must change?
feedback.strategy_support: Use EXACT FACTORS → KNOWN ANCHOR/COMMUTATIVE ARRAY → ADD OR REMOVE COMPLETE GROUPS → INVERSE OR ARRAY CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.

question_variants.explanation: 120 + 24 = 144, so 12 × 12 = 144. This derives the fact instead of counting from one.

feedback.correct: Relay secured using spaced derived fact retrieval.
feedback.repair: Turn the array or start from the shown anchor fact, adjust by complete equal groups and verify the product.
feedback.misconception_check: Use the known anchor instead of recounting every group from one.
feedback.retry: The forge keeps your correct steps. Choose another strategy and continue without a timer or penalty.
feedback.representation_evidence: 12 equal groups of 12 make 144; turning the array gives 12 groups of 12 with the same product.
feedback.check_prompt: Which known rows can stay, and exactly which complete rows must be added to reach the target array?
feedback.strategy_support: Use EXACT FACTORS → KNOWN ANCHOR/COMMUTATIVE ARRAY → ADD OR REMOVE COMPLETE GROUPS → INVERSE OR ARRAY CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Arrays, equal-group trays, fact families, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, fine dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.

body.prompt: A navigator says 12 × 12 = 132. What is the correct product?

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses nearby multiplication facts such as 6 x 8 and 7 x 8.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Wrong answer is one row of the repeated factor away from the correct product.
repair_sequence: Ask the child to explain why the product increased by 8.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Child answers the related fact pair and names the row difference.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Counts every fact from one instead of using known facts.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Long response time with repeated near-correct counting evidence.
repair_sequence: Build from the anchor with one or two jumps.
Repeat with a new fact using the same strategy.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Child uses an anchor fact without counting from one.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Does not connect division facts to multiplication facts.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Correct multiplication but incorrect inverse division task.
repair_sequence: Ask which factor is missing when the product and other factor are known.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Child solves matching multiplication and division pairs.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use still array transitions, no flashing forge effects and quiet success glow.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace orbit and snap animations with instant state changes and subtle outline changes.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read prompts aloud and let the child replay factor/product vocabulary.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use short labels, icon-backed choices and narrated worked examples.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Split the mission into warm-up, build, checkpoint and teach-back with visible progress.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Disable timers until secure recall is established.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use repair-first language and celebrate strategy use, not just speed.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach factor, product, rows, columns and equal groups with visuals.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires 80% accuracy across at least two formats with reduced hints; secure requires retained success across mixed facts and related division.
evidence.parent_summary: Can recall mixed multiplication and division facts with increasing fluency and can use patterns to repair tricky facts.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Accuracy, speed, strategy explanation, inverse fact connection and reduced hint use across timed recall, array build and division match.
next_actions: Interleave area arrays when multiplication recall is secure.
Route to row-difference repair for near-table confusions.
Schedule spaced retrieval at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

qa.notes: Quality-hardened 240-item Year 4 multiplication pilot preserving the exact 126 legacy and 114 deterministic depth membership, IDs, answers, blueprint allocation, arithmetic and 12x12 scope. All legacy variants now have concept-specific correct feedback, array/equal-groups/fact-family evidence, repair and misconception/check prompts; existing depth feedback and pressure-free missions are retained and supplemented with the same evidence contract. Every variant has explicit touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC/point/adult-scribed routes with no mandatory fine dragging, handwriting or speech. No speed scoring, timers, streak loss or retry cost are introduced. Narration remains selectively absent; future narration must use produced, human-reviewed ElevenLabs assets and browser TTS is prohibited. Independent mathematics, teacher, accessibility, safeguarding and renderer review remain required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
